highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.195 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS to disable mouse interactions in fullscreen mode while keeping scroll wheel support. It also fixes a bug where hook matchers with hyphenated identifiers (like 'code-reviewer' or 'mcp__brave-search') were accidentally matching substrings instead of exact names — important if you rely on hooks for automation.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.193 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces the autoMode.classifyAllShell setting, which routes all Bash and PowerShell commands through the auto-mode classifier — not just commands flagged as arbitrary code execution. Auto-mode denial reasons are now shown in the transcript, the denial toast, and under /permissions recent denials, making it much easier to understand why a command was blocked.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.191 Released
highlights.summary: A handy new /rewind command lets you resume a conversation from before a /clear was run, so you no longer lose context after accidentally clearing your session. This release also fixes scroll position jumping to the bottom while reading earlier output during streaming, and stops background agents from restarting after being manually stopped.

highlights.title: The Text in Claude Code's 'Extended Thinking' Output Is Not Authentic
highlights.summary: A developer explores how the visible 'thinking' text shown during Claude Code's Extended Thinking mode may not accurately represent the model's actual internal reasoning process. This is a useful read for developers who rely on the thinking output to debug or audit Claude's decisions, as it suggests the displayed text should be treated as an approximate summary rather than a literal trace.

highlights.title: Show HN: Recall – Local Project Memory for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Recall is an open-source tool that gives Claude Code persistent, local memory about your project — storing context that survives across sessions without sending data to any external server. This is especially useful for large codebases where you want Claude to remember architectural decisions, naming conventions, or recurring tasks without re-explaining them every time.

highlights.title: Agent Rigor – Stop Your AI Coding Assistant from Doom-Looping
highlights.summary: Agent Rigor is an open-source tool designed to detect and break the 'doom loop' pattern where AI coding agents keep retrying failed approaches in circles, wasting tokens and time. It provides guardrails and intervention hooks you can integrate with Claude Code to catch runaway agent behavior early and redirect it.

highlights.title: 60-Minute Complete Claude Code Masterclass (with Full Documentation)
highlights.summary: A comprehensive Bilibili tutorial covering Claude Code from initial installation through advanced usage patterns, complete with accompanying written documentation. Produced over three weeks, it is one of the most thorough Chinese-language resources available for developers in mainland China looking to get productive with Claude Code quickly.

highlights.title: Claude Code - Full Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: A full beginner-focused YouTube tutorial walking through Claude Code setup and core workflows step by step. It is a good starting point if you are new to AI-assisted coding and want a single video that covers the essentials without assuming prior experience.

tips_of_the_day: If you accidentally run /clear and lose your session context, use the new /rewind command (v2.1.191+) to restore the conversation to the state it was in before the clear — no need to re-explain your project from scratch.

When writing hooks that target MCP tools or custom reviewers with hyphenated names (e.g. 'mcp__brave-search'), double-check your hook matchers after upgrading to v2.1.195 — they now require exact matches, so any wildcards or partial strings that worked before may silently stop firing.

Enable autoMode.classifyAllShell in your Claude Code settings (v2.1.193+) if you want consistent auto-mode behavior across all shell commands, not just those flagged as high-risk — then check /permissions recent denials to understand exactly why any command was blocked before trying to rewrite it.
